title: Background
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter B covering Taylor series expansions, integration using series, and complex roots. Problems include expressing functions like e^(-x^2), e^(-x^3), and cos(x^2) as Taylor series and using truncated series to evaluate definite integrals, as well as finding series expansions for specific functions and expressing polynomials as Taylor series about a given expansion point.

figures.caption: A circle with four marked points connected by two intersecting chords, illustrating the geometric solution set of a system of equations.
